title: Students' mental health skills CV
description: A co-created initiative to integrate mental health and wellbeing as a fundamental skill in schools. It embeds wellbeing literacy into existing curricula, connects it with employability skills (“skills CVs”), and involves students as peer advocates.

title: Digital wellbeing lab
description: A collaborative space where schools, youth, and tech mentors co-design digital tools and campaigns (apps, TikTok challenges, social media storytelling) that promote healthy online behaviour and mental wellbeing.

title: Community of practice: Teachers as change agents
description: A professional training program for teachers focused on mental health literacy, early identification, and referral pathways. It builds peer learning networks and integrates wellbeing support into continuous professional development.

title: Transition to adulthood pathways
description: A coordinated initiative between the public, private, and community sectors that empowers young people to design their own transition from school to higher education or employment. The prototype seeks to strengthen the interconnections between the education, employment, and mental health systems, ensuring that young people receive holistic support during this critical life stage. The pathways are co-created through a shared learning space involving key actors within Ireland’s mental health ecosystem. Continuous feedback and periodic reviews are embedded using the developmental evaluation approach, allowing the model to adapt and improve over time based on real-time insights.

title: Cross-sector residencies
description: Short-term residencies allowing public servants to work within community mental health projects — and community leaders to spend time inside public institutions — promoting empathy, systemic understanding, and joint problem-solving. This initiative promotes collaboration across local–regional–national levels and rural-urban areas.

title: Adopt a practice
description: A funding (through micro-grants) and mentorship mechanism that enables organisations to adapt proven mental health initiatives from other Irish regions or international contexts, fostering replication and systemic diffusion.
